04/13/2026 Alvy Gordo:   Fully executed amendment received. 
- Requested O2C to create a new draft subscription with a 2-year term to proceed with provisioning, as the extension license is currently encountering issues - https://devgraph-alp.atlassian.net/browse/O2C-86164
- Requested License provisioning for 2 yrs to be sent to support for licensing purposes - https://central-supportdesk.kayako.com/agent/conversations/60347784 

04/13/2026 Tim Courtenay:  We have received the fully executed document, I have attached it. Sales Ops to do the following:
- extend the existing subscription by 2 years. This is important - we need to reflect this as a 3yr agreement, not a 2 yr agreement. The following renewal opp needs to pick up a 3yr contract term.
- On 3/10, Brad Cron confirmed that they do not need to raise a PO, we can simply invoice them.
- rapidly raise a license renewal ticket for an additional 2 years. Note that support are for some reason struggling with the temp license extension: cancel that and focus their attention on the full license extension.
- raise an invoice request with O2C.

04/12/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Support say they can't find the license info to process the temp extension. I replied to say they already did this 2 weeks ago, just do the same thing again. 

04/12/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Asked legal if the agreement has been signed.

2026-04-10 SYSTEM : Renewal Extension ticket Link: https://central-supportdesk.kayako.com/agent/conversations/60346162

4/10/2026 Tim Courtenay - Renewal extension requested :-
Customer committed date: 2026-04-23
Extension Reason: The Amendment has been signed by the customer, submitted to Artie for our signature. Not appropriate to send the extension request as they have already contractually committed to the 2-year extension. I have attached the email for reference.

04/10/2026 Tim Courtenay: I replied to Brad's note about the software expiring tomorrow to confirm the amendment has been approved by legal and is awaiting our counter-signature. I flagged a key open item — whether Netcare needs to raise a PO before we can invoice and renew — and asked for urgent clarification. I also committed to requesting a temporary license extension to relieve the deadline pressure.

Submitted extension request.

04/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Forwarded Brad's signed amendment copy to legal and advised Brad that legal has not yet processed it but expects to hear back today.

04/08/2026 Tim Courtenay: Brad explained that Travis declined to sign via AdobeSign due to Netcare's internal governance process requiring their own approval flow. Brad has sent a signed copy from their internal process and asked if we can countersign on that basis.


04/08/2026 Tim Courtenay: Followed up with Brad to check whether Travis received the AdobeSign amendment document from legal, as the temporary license extension expiry date is approaching. Also asked legal to confirm that the document was actually signed and sent by us. Brad confirmed the amendment is currently making its way through Netcare's internal legal sign-off process — he has completed his sign-off and is waiting on others, expecting it to be returned to us shortly for final signature.

04/05/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Brad is OoO. Requested legal to get the amendment signed and sent to the customer as we are way past the renewal date now. Advised Brad by email.

04/02/2026 Tim Courtenay: Followed up with Brad requesting approval on the updated amendment draft, with the goal of getting it back to legal today to send to Netcare for signature.

03/30/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Sent updated Amendment to Brad Cron; asked for confirmation we can go to signature.

2026-03-30 SYSTEM : Renewal Extension ticket Link: https://central-supportdesk.kayako.com/agent/conversations/60336696

3/30/2026 Tim Courtenay - Renewal extension requested :-
Customer committed date: 2026-04-10
Extension Reason: We (legal) have delayed getting the amendment out to the customer. Renewal is in 2 days. I have not asked the customer for an extension form - this is on us. Attached the legal case.

03/26/2026 Tim Courtenay: Brad (Netcare) flagged that Section 3 (Fees) in the amendment is ambiguous — the $246,925.74 total could be misread as due over the remaining 2 years rather than the full 3-year term at $82,308.58/year. He requested the wording be updated to clearly show the annual breakdown, periods, amounts already paid, and amounts remaining. I confirmed I would ask legal to make the change.

03/25/2026 Tim Courtenay:  The updated Amendment is in finalization, there were a few quality problems with it. Advised what correction needs to be made, requested an update.

03/23/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Brad confirmed their CIO is on board. Followed up with legal to ensure they have the correct brief for the Amendment. We need this today to get it signed and the renewal processed before end of month.

03/20/2026 Tim Courtenay: Advised Brad that the draft contract amendment is delayed due to a backlog in legal — targeting Monday delivery. Also followed up to confirm whether Brad has discussed the situation with the CIO (Travis Dewing).

03/19/2026 Alvy Gordo:   "We have agreed to extend the customer's existing one year subscription to a three year subscription, following an escalation from the customer and a review of the circumstances at the last renewal."  - https://trilogy-sales.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Case/500fu00000S2FvhAAF/view

03/18/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Follow-up call with Brad. We agreed that a simple Amendment to extend the term fo the existing agreement by an additional 2 years would be easiest. Get it to him asap. He has sent details of the signatory (Travis Dewing, CIO), we can send to Travis, copy Brad, from AdobeSign.

Created Case to request Amendment.

03/17/2026 Tim Courtenay: Brad confirmed a meeting for tomorrow (Wed 18 Mar) at 12:30 to discuss the options for converting the current 1-yr contract to a 3-yr term. I replied to confirm acceptance.

03/16/2026 Tim Courtenay: Brad missed the 9am Monday call. I followed up requesting 30 mins any time after 10am Tuesday. Context: a 2yr extension at existing pricing ($82k) has been approved internally. Need to align with Brad on the administrative mechanism to execute this — options are: (1) new 3yr quote, (2) 2yr extension with a note confirming 3yr term, or (3) amendment to the signed quote.

03/13/2026 Alvy Gordo: Created  quote Q-47864 for 24 months in preparation for flat invoicing  
Item:  DNN Prod - 4 
DNN Domain 2 
DNN Non Prod - 2

Term:    24 mos 

03/13/2026 Tim Courtenay: Followed up with Brad to arrange a same-day call to finalize the 2-year contract extension at existing pricing ($82k/year). Noted that administrative steps and documentation (likely a backdated 3yr renewal quote or 2yr extension agreement) need to be discussed before instructions can be issued to process. Proposed a call between 1–3pm today.

03/12/2026 Tim Courtenay:  requesting formal note from SVP approving the extension decision, then we will process with O2C. Received approval, attached it to the files tab. Requested Sales Ops to process the extension to the subscription.

03/10/2026 Tim Courtenay: Brad followed up asking for an update on his request. I confirmed the request is under review with a decision expected by Wednesday. Brad acknowledged. Later the same day, I informed Brad that a 2-year extension on the existing contract and pricing has been approved. Next step is to determine the administrative mechanism — either a backdated 3yr renewal quote or a 2yr extension agreement — and follow up within 1-2 days.

03/10/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Approval from SVP to extend the customer's existing 1 yr term to 3 yrs on the basis that was clearly the intent at last renewal. 

03/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Brad followed up asking for news on his request (to reinstate the 3-year term at the original ~$82k/year rate). I replied that the request is under internal review and we expect a decision by Wednesday.

03/04/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Brad sent an email motivation through an encrypted message. Replied that we will look at this request and respond. The email extract is as below. Raising this as an internal escalation, will review with the SVP.
---
"HI Tim, Hope you doing well.
As discussed in some of our previous conversations, I would like to formally request that this matter be escalated and logged with the appropriate internal teams for review.
I was quite concerned to learn that our 2025 renewal is currently reflected as a one‑year contract, as I have always requested that our renewal be processed on a three‑year or five-year term.To support this, I have attached all communication records on my side, which I believe are also available in your CRM system. At no point did I indicate a change in preference or agree to move to an annual contract.
At the time of signing, the documentation reflected a three‑year renewal clause; however, as you explained, the period is driven by the top term date, which in this case appears a little ambiguous.
“Your auto-renewed term is set to begin 1-Apr-2025. Per the applicable auto-renewal clause in your current agreement, your subscription will automatically renew for another 36 months at the rate of USD 193,149.52.”
Given this, I am currently in a difficult position, as no budget was allocated for an annual renewal increase—especially not at the significantly increased amount proposed, from roughly $ 82k to $ 113k per annum.
I would sincerely appreciate your assistance in having this matter reviewed urgently so that we can resolve it appropriately, as you know we have been a DNN customer for many years, and we don’t intend on changing that.
Thank you in advance for your support.
Regards
Brad Cron
---

02/17/2026 Tim Courtenay: Followed up on stalled DNN renewal. Brad has not provided promised formal documentation regarding 1-year vs. 3-year contract term dispute. Awaiting his decision on contract term, pricing ($102.9k annually or 3-year ramped option), and Platinum Success interest. End of February deadline to prevent auto-renewal processing. Brad replied, said he will reply by Friday.

02/10/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Sent follow-up 'call to action' email to Brad. ~2 weeks to AR.

01/30/2026 Tim Courtenay: Follow-up call with Brad Cron (Netcare) regarding DNN renewal. Brad believes the 2025 signed 12-month contract was meant to be a 3-year deal and provided evidence supporting that. Tim explained that any changes would require a formal customer motivation and legal review. Discussed possible pricing flexibility and customer request for a lower rate. Clarified contract language and renewal terms.
Actions:
* Brad to send formal motivation with email evidence (due by Feb 3)
* Tim to log case and raise internally
* Tim to review possibility of flexible pricing model or discount
Follow up meeting to booked depending on outcome.

01/27/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Brad booked a meeting for 1/30.

01/26/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Brad Cron emailed to claim they are entitled to a 3 year term on their current quote. Countered that with a detailed email and requested a meeting to unblock this and move forward.

01/12/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Send a follow-up email to Brad and Lance, requested a meeting to agree next steps and address any concerns and questions.

11/20/2025 Tim Courtenay: Customer is a high-value account using DNN Evoq for critical patient/doctor portals and is satisfied with the platform stability. A discrepancy exists regarding the contract term; Customer believes they signed a 3-year renewal (2025-2028) at ~$82k/year, while our records show a 1-year term. Customer is open to a 3-year renewal to lock in pricing. They are cautious about AI due to healthcare regulations but interested in back-office use cases. 
Actions:
1. Generate 1 yr and 3yr Standard Success quotes.
2. Send quotes, plus supporting info for Platinum, AI and Prime to Brad.
3. Send both by 11/26
4. Follow up meeting will be set after that. 
I sent a meeting follow-up email.
